items.caption:
Top attractions in Banff, Canada and the total cost of the trip during peak summer season (4 nights, family of 3) 🌲🏔️ It’s one of the most breathtaking places, would highly recommend a visit :)

As someone who didn’t go on vacations that often growing up, it brings me a lot of joy to be able to treat my parents now 🥰🥹

@hotelcanoeandsuites @banffgondola @suddensally 

#travel #personalfinance #banff #canada #money
items.transcript: Banff is one of the most breathtaking places I've ever been and this is the cost of a four-night trip for a family of three and the attractions you must see. We stayed four nights at Hotel Canoe & Suites for around $2,500. I love the cabin vibe and the heated outdoor pool. Flights on WestJet came out to around $1,300. If I could pick only one attraction to go to, it would be Lake Louise. It is insane how clear and pristine the water looks. We used a tour service but you definitely don't have to. If I were to get married one day, I think this is a great location. Of course, a Banff trip wouldn't be complete without kayaking. Johnston Canyon is an easy hike and completely free because there's so much snow on the mountain calves that's where a lot of the water comes from. Another attraction I loved was the Banff Gondola. It's a scenic ride to the top of Sulphur Mountain and it is a sweeping view of the Canadian Rockies. You can enjoy dining up there as well as a museum. We rented a car to get around for the trip and that cost us $655. Food for the whole trip was around $500. My favorite restaurant was called Sud & Sally. This brings the grand total of the trip to $6,000 or around $2,000 per person. As someone who didn't go on a lot of vacations growing up, it brings me a lot of joy to take my parents on new experiences.
